---
title: "An initial exploration of body-mind interaction: Effects of BMAA on body sensation, working memory, and attentional control"
lang: en
language: ../../assets/language-en.yml
toc-title: "On this page"
---

## Method

A child pretest-posttest control-group study, with additional baseline analyses examining relations between body awareness and executive functions.

## Key Findings

- Children with better proprioceptive awareness tended to show better sustained attention at baseline.
- Compared with the control group, BMAA-C showed greater gains in working memory and sustained attention, with effects moderated by baseline bodily-sensation ability.

## Reference

李少揚. (2017). *身心互動途徑初探：以「身心中軸覺察訓練」對身體感覺、工作記憶與注意力控制功能的提升效果為例* [碩士論文，國立臺灣大學]. https://doi.org/10.6342/NTU201700254
